---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
##library(readxl)
##library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size55[.center[
Continue to develop and refine our operationalization of terrorism
]]

<br>

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

### Prep for Class
1. Focus most of today's work on the definitions, leave only a handful of minutes for the Dylann Roof discussion

<br>

On Monday we explored a variety of cases in an effort to bolster our intuitions about terrorism and to help us clarify the concept itself.

- Specifically we built a list of common characteristics, identified sub-groupings and created a list of important questions.

For today you read a short article by Charles Tilly who is one of the preeminent scholars studying terrorism today.

- A mere sociologist, but we won't hold that against him! :)

Tilly (2004) offers a lot of useful critical analysis of the exercise we are working through: Defining terrorism.

<br>

Everybody form groups of 3, create space between you and try to work with new people! 
- Mix it up!



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size55[.center[**Terrorism**]]

.size45[
1. **Define the Concept**
    - What does terrorism mean in the abstract?

2. **Operationalize the Concept**
    - What "observable phenomena" should represent it?
]

???

Groups you now have 20 minutes to use our case study work from Monday AND the Tilly (2004) argument to answer these two questions.

<br>

Don't over-think this.
- This is almost exactly the exercise we did on Monday.
- The list of common characteristics represents your options for the defintion.
- The sub-groups and questions represent the observable phenomena.

Your job is to pick the pieces you found most compelling.

In 20 minutes you will write your definition AND list of observable phenomena on the board.

### Is everybody clear on what I am asking for?

Get to work!

<br>

*ON BOARD*: Definitions; Operationalizations
- PRESENT and DISCUSS each

<br>

*Prior class definitional work*
- (Maximize / Destabilize) use of (violence/harm/actions to cause fear) by non-state actor for (political/strategic/ideological/cause fear/change SQ) purposes targeting specific (people/group/idea).




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/10-1-terrorism_word_cloud.jpg')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

???

### How does Tilly feel about our effort to create a single, concise definition of terrorism? (p9)
- ("No useful generalization covers all the different sorts of political interaction for which observers, analysts, and participants sometimes use the term terror, much less for terrorists and terrorism" (9).)

### Is he right? Why or why not?

Sure it's possible, but that's not a deal-breaker for us.

1. He's saying there's no consensus across all "observers, analysts, and participants," not that we can't define the concept.
    - Let's not assume all of those perspectives are equal.

2. We have huge needs to be able to identify and measure terrorism so we need a clear place to start.
    - Can't address it if we can't measure it.
    
<br>

The goal for us as social scientists is to be able to clearly communicate A SINGLE compelling definition for terrorism, NOT create THE ONLY definition.

That definition then guides us as we measure terrorism in the world.



---

background-image: url('libs/Images/background-blue_triangles2.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center
class: middle

.size55[.center[**The Concept of Terrorism (Tilly 2004)**]]

<br>

.size45[
1. Terror as a strategy 

2. Multiple uses of terror 

3. Terror and other forms of struggle 

4. Terror and specialists in coercion
]

???

### What do you think of Tilly's four part attempt to define terrorism?

#### - How did this influence your thinking?

<br>

### And according to Tilly, who are the three audiences of the terror signal?
1. (The targets themselves)
2. (potential allies of the perpetrators, and)
3. (third parties that might cooperate with one or the other.)

<br>

### For Friday: Take home all class definitions and work to combine them into one!







---

background-image: url('libs/Images/10_2-dylann_roof1.webp')
background-size: 70%
background-position: right
class: middle, slideblue

.pull-left[

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

<br>

.size90[.center[.textred[**Is Dylann Roof a terrorist?**]]]

]

???

I know this case is getting old but I think it is an excellent way to try and use our definitions / operationalizations on what appears to be a particularly difficult case to define.

### Based on everything we've read and discussed, is Dylann Roof a terorrist? Why or why not?

*Force this debate: make sure each side gets made and explored*

*ON BOARD:* "YES" ARGUMENT vs "NO" ARGUMENT




---

background-image: url('libs/Images/10_2-roof_poll.png')
background-size: 100%
background-position: center

???

To complicate matters...

Here we see polling data on the Dylann Roof question.

- CNN/ORC International Poll (July 2, 2015)

<br>

### What does this poll indicate about wider public opinion?

<br>

### Should public opinion polling matter for how we think about this case?

#### - Why or why not?
